notes_md:
**căng** = tense, tight, under pressure. It can describe feelings, people, situations, or even physical things pulled tight.

As an adjective, it goes after the noun and describes emotional stress (tâm trạng căng) or a serious atmosphere. It also describes something physically tight or stretched, like skin, muscles, rope, or clothes.

Very common collocations: **căng thẳng** (more formal/clear “stressed”), **căng não** (mentally intense), **căng cơ** (muscle strain).  

Think of **căng** vs **thoải mái / thư giãn** (relaxed) — strong contrast to remember it.
exampleSentencesTranslated: The atmosphere in the meeting room this morning is very tense because everyone is arguing.
I feel the situation between those two people is becoming more tense.
Don't talk right now, mom is very tense after her work shift.

definitions.vi: Căng hoặc kéo dài; căng thẳng.

notes:
căng = tense, tight, under pressure. It can describe feelings, people, situations, or even physical things pulled tight.

As an adjective, it goes after the noun and describes emotional stress (tâm trạng căng) or a serious atmosphere. It also describes something physically tight or stretched, like skin, muscles, rope, or clothes.

Very common collocations: căng thẳng (more formal/clear “stressed”), căng não (mentally intense), căng cơ (muscle strain).  

Think of căng vs thoải mái / thư giãn (relaxed) — strong contrast to remember it.
exampleSentences: Không khí trong phòng họp sáng nay rất căng vì mọi người tranh luận.
Em thấy tình hình giữa hai người họ đang trở nên căng hơn rồi.
Anh đừng nói chuyện vào lúc này, mẹ đang rất căng sau buổi làm việc.
